findings.summary: _update_paperless_document manually discards pre/processing tags and adds post tag instead of using clean_pipeline_tags like the classifier worker does
evidence: Lines 197-200: current_tags.discard(self.settings.PRE_TAG_ID); if self.settings.OCR_PROCESSING_TAG_ID: current_tags.discard(...); current_tags.add(self.settings.POST_TAG_ID) — ad-hoc tag surgery instead of using clean_pipeline_tags as classifier/worker.py _apply_classification does at line 278
findings.suggestion: Use clean_pipeline_tags(current_tags, self.settings) then add POST_TAG_ID, matching the classifier worker pattern and centralizing tag-transition logic.

findings.summary: _has_ocr_errors checks only the single REFUSAL_MARK string instead of the full OCR_REFUSAL_MARKERS list used during per-page OCR
evidence: Line 173: is_error_content(text, (self.settings.REFUSAL_MARK,)) passes only REFUSAL_MARK, while OcrProvider.transcribe_image at provider.py line 107 checks self.settings.OCR_REFUSAL_MARKERS which includes all REFUSAL_PHRASES plus REFUSAL_MARK
findings.suggestion: Pass self.settings.OCR_REFUSAL_MARKERS to is_error_content so the post-assembly check uses the same phrase set as per-page refusal detection.

findings.summary: update_document_metadata accepts **fields: Any and silently drops unknown keys, masking caller typos at runtime
evidence: Lines 168-182: the method accepts **fields: Any and filters through _METADATA_FIELDS with 'if key in self._METADATA_FIELDS'. A caller passing 'correspondant_id' (typo) gets no error and no update. DocumentMetadataUpdate TypedDict exists but is not enforced.
findings.suggestion: Log a warning or raise TypeError for keys not in _METADATA_FIELDS, or use Unpack[DocumentMetadataUpdate] to get static checking.

findings.summary: retry decorator wrapper has no explicit return after the for-loop, making the unreachable path invisible to type checkers
evidence: Lines 32-56: the for-loop re-raises on the last attempt so post-loop code is unreachable, but the function signature claims -> T while the implicit fall-through returns None
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit 'raise AssertionError("unreachable")' after the for-loop to satisfy type checkers and document the invariant.

findings.summary: Both daemons create a new PaperlessClient (and underlying httpx.Client with TCP connection setup) for every single document processed
evidence: ocr/daemon.py line 20-21: PaperlessClient(settings) created fresh per document
classifier/daemon.py line 67: PaperlessClient(settings) created fresh per document
findings.suggestion: Use a thread-local or per-worker PaperlessClient rather than per-document creation. httpx.Client is thread-safe so a shared instance per thread-pool worker would reduce connection churn without sacrificing isolation.

findings.summary: Settings class uses instance attributes set in __init__ but declares them as class-level annotations that look like class variables, creating a misleading dual layer
evidence: Lines 17-60: class-level annotations like 'PAPERLESS_URL: str' and 'LLM_PROVIDER: Literal["openai", "ollama"]' suggest these are class attributes, but they are actually instance attributes set in __init__ sub-methods. The REFUSAL_MARK at line 51 has a default value making it a true class variable, inconsistent with the others.
findings.suggestion: Use __slots__ or a dataclass/attrs pattern to clarify these are instance attributes. At minimum, move REFUSAL_MARK's default into __init__ for consistency.
